title: Chronik coding memory and Vibe-Lab effect pilot
goal: Make Chronik a narrow local coding-outcome memory for Grabowski and test prospectively in Vibe-Lab whether a frozen history brief improves real coding decisions without becoming live truth or automatic policy.
completion: Chronik imports explicit coding outcomes locally and idempotently and can freeze hash-bound history cohorts.
Grabowski records the actual target repository and exposes bounded import and historical query surfaces.
Vibe-Lab contains a valid prospective control/treatment registration with no fabricated observations.
Systemkatalog maps active learning evidence to Vibe-Lab and retains Heimlern only as historical reference.
The natural-case pilot is closed with promote, pilot, defer, reject or archive without automatic policy mutation.
